content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


July 18, 2001


Steve Hartsell, Legal Advisor
California's Coalition for Adequate
School Housing
1130 K Street, Suite 210
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-01-134

Dear Mr. Hartsell:

This correspondence is written confirmation that the Fair Political Practices Commission
("FPPC") has withdrawn your May 30, 2001 request for advice under the provisions of the
Political Reform Act¹.

As advised in my June 13, 2001 letter to you, unless you are an authorized representative
of the citizens' oversight committee for which you seek assistance, the FPPC cannot address
your questions or provide analysis. I enclose a copy of Regulation 18329 for your edification.

Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel

By: Jody Feldman
Legal Division

JF:jg
Enclosure

1 Government Code sections 81000- 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of
the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC can provide advice on Political Reform Act matters to a legal advisor who may not be an authorized representative of the citizens' oversight committee in question.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has withdrawn the request for advice because the requester does not appear to be an authorized representative of the citizens' oversight committee for which assistance was sought, and therefore the FPPC cannot address the questions or provide analysis under Regulation 18329.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter rather than an advice letter. No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in standard format. The document confirms withdrawal of a prior request based on lack of standing/authorization. Enclosure reference to Regulation 18329 suggests eligibility requirements for seeking FPPC advice.

QUESTION: Whether the FPPC can provide advice on Political Reform Act matters to a legal advisor who may not be an authorized representative of the citizens' oversight committee in question.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC has withdrawn the request for advice because the requester does not appear to be an authorized representative of the citizens' oversight committee for which assistance was sought, and therefore the FPPC cannot address the questions or provide analysis under Regulation 18329.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew a May 30, 2001 advice request from Steve Hartsell, legal advisor for California's Coalition for Adequate School Housing, because he lacks authorization to represent the citizens' oversight committee at issue.
